[2018-12-23 03:39] mbingo: I think the fact that inflation is a well-respected concept in Prismata is a decent argument for the relevance of math in the game.  Yeah, it's not like you're doing those calculations during the game, but it seems like an important thing to study or attempt to grasp if you want to get _good_.
[2018-12-23 03:40] mbingo: I also get that it's not what most people are referring to when they throw around "math game", but I think they coincidentally have a leg to stand on.  (In addition the "math and logic are related" argument, and I think Prismata is absolutely a game of logic.)
[2018-12-23 03:40] mbingo: Agreed though that it's kind of silly for people to call it a math game if they're referring to calculating attack/defense etc.
[2018-12-23 03:40] Deleted User (Deleted User): it's like saying you should be good at numbers/math to drive a racecar
[2018-12-23 03:42] mbingo: I think reading and understanding, say, Yujiri's page on inflation probably requires some decent math understanding and probably helps you become a better player.  Not sure if I see the analog in racecar driving.
[2018-12-23 03:43] mbingo: But again, you can get good without really doing any actual math, but there are valuable concepts deep in the game that get pretty mathematical, I think.
[2018-12-23 03:44] Deleted User (Deleted User): u don't actually need to know the exact inflation numbers just the concept of whether it's low/mid/high inflation
[2018-12-23 03:45] mbingo: No, obviously not.  But it's probably helpful to know how to think about these things so you can intuitively estimate, no?  No one's memorizing relative inflation values.  (... right?)
[2018-12-23 03:46] Deleted User (Deleted User): yeah see above about good numbers/math racecar
[2018-12-23 03:46] mbingo: You're trying to figure out how to value different units, and the idea of inflation is a helpful tool.  It just feels to me a little disingenuous when people say "oh come on there's no math in this thing I don't get it".
[2018-12-23 03:46] zera777 (Zera): Literally all games are math
[2018-12-23 03:47] mbingo: Though I know I'm not doing any serious math when I play, I certainly feel that same part of my brain working.  It doesn't need to be literally mathy for it to be a reasonable comment to make.
[2018-12-23 03:47] zera777 (Zera): Of course, Prismata is a turn based game, which means you have more time to appreciate it.
[2018-12-23 03:47] f300xen (f0x): inflation is just "things generate value over time" in a less abstract way
[2018-12-23 03:48] mbingo: Okay, but reading that sentence doesn't make someone suddenly understand inflation in Prismata.

[2018-12-23 07:14] mbingo: Yeah, I think the critical point was mentioned—I'd be surprised to hear anyone say there's difficult math (arithmetic) involved in playing Prismata, but just a lot of it.  You're constantly doing those little calculations, trying to squeeze value out of every bit of gold.  If you want to look one turn ahead and figure out what you might be able to buy, you're doing these little gold-sums.  Oh, you want to keep your patrollers/sentries, so subtract that out of the given defense number.  Ah, I'm hurtin for granularity... the defense calc is wrinkled a bit.  Hmm, they have a couple nivos and a frostbite, but they can't use all that chill, so lemme figure this out.  Yeah, it's all somewhat trivial arithmetic, but I think it's defensible for a prospective player to not really feel super into that aspect of gameplay.
[2018-12-23 07:14] mbingo: Sorry, a bit of a speech.

[2018-12-26 19:11] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): Hey, I signed up during Alpha and I haven't played for a while, just getting back into Prismata now
[2018-12-26 19:12] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): Not entirely sure I remember how it works too well
[2018-12-26 19:13] siepu: Play campaign it works as tutorial!
[2018-12-26 19:13] siepu: Then 'battle training' it's a tutorial too
[2018-12-26 19:14] siepu: Watch @amalloy videos in bed, best falling asleep material on the web
[2018-12-26 19:14] siepu: You will both have great sleep and learn a lot too
[2018-12-26 19:15] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): I opened up a power core and flipped some cards
[2018-12-26 19:15] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): How do I know which prizes to pick?
[2018-12-26 19:16] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): Do I go for highest rarity?
[2018-12-26 19:16] siepu: Whichever you like as you won't get any anyway
[2018-12-26 19:16] amalloy: there are no interesting tricks for optimizing the armory
[2018-12-26 19:16] amalloy: just pick stuff you think is cool
[2018-12-26 19:17] amalloy: personally i'm a fan of the googly-eyes/natural/army skin-sets
[2018-12-26 19:17] awaclus: googly eyes is the best
[2018-12-26 19:17] siepu: Googly eyes antima #1
[2018-12-26 19:18] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): Oh that's one of my options in the second power core
[2018-12-26 19:18] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): Eyes-On-Target Antima Comet
[2018-12-26 19:18] amalloy: by the way, when was the free weekend on steam before f2p? was it in like may?
[2018-12-26 19:19] siepu: May or June that's when I got the game
[2018-12-26 19:19] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): Is the currency used to flip extra cards difficult to earn?
[2018-12-26 19:19] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): Should I bother flipping more or nah?
[2018-12-26 19:19] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): I'll just take the emotes then
[2018-12-26 19:20] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): 4 off googly eyes comet
[2018-12-26 19:20] siepu: Skins are super whatever - and that's what I meant you won't get anything anyway
[2018-12-26 19:20] awaclus: >Is the currency used to flip extra cards difficult to earn?
you earn 15 per day by playing the dailies, another 5+ a day from ranked tickets, and a few hundred as event rewards
[2018-12-26 19:20] siepu: Power cores are really easy to get but 90% of them are just emotes
[2018-12-26 19:20] awaclus: I would flip for a chance to get googly eyes but that's just me
[2018-12-26 19:21] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): I already pressed done
[2018-12-26 19:21] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): There goes googly eyes
[2018-12-26 19:21] siepu: Prismata is a game of morale and Google eyes antima is an absolute morale killer
[2018-12-26 19:21] awaclus: in the long term, it's probably easy enough to acquire them through omnipower cores without flipping
[2018-12-26 19:21] siepu: You don't get omnis unless you play rankeds
[2018-12-26 19:22] awaclus: and more useful to save shards for reflipping in the black lab
[2018-12-26 19:22] amalloy: @siepu exchange at the black lab
[2018-12-26 19:22] awaclus: you get an option to exchange 40 power cores for 3 black cards and 3 omnis once you have collected enough low-level rewards
[2018-12-26 19:22] siepu: Black lab flipping is pointless tho as you just reshuffle
[2018-12-26 19:22] amalloy: god if i had to spend power cores in the armory i would go crazy
[2018-12-26 19:23] siepu: Scource: spent 2k in black lab to reshuffle got a random skin every time
[2018-12-26 19:23] amalloy: @sami 🥪 it's just a chore to press "yes okay go yes okay go" N times for your N power cores, to get a bunch of emotes i don't care about
[2018-12-26 19:23] siepu: Didn't know about the power core exchange, thanks
[2018-12-26 19:24] amalloy: but after you do that enough times they say "hey, you can trade 40 power cores for a smaller number of more potent things"
[2018-12-26 19:24] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): I like that "Recent internet search queries" section of the character bio
[2018-12-26 19:24] awaclus: >Black lab flipping is pointless tho as you just reshuffle
it isn't pointless
[2018-12-26 19:25] awaclus: getting another chance at getting good RNG is good
[2018-12-26 19:25] siepu: I just thing it's more effective to spend purple money when opening the cores
[2018-12-26 19:25] sami.mp3 (sami 🥪): "why does cilantro taste like soap"
[2018-12-26 19:25] siepu: Told ya it's decently written :)
[2018-12-26 19:25] awaclus: in theory, if you want to collect every collectible item in the game, rerolling in black lab is the best way to spend shards
[2018-12-26 19:26] awaclus: if you only care about Prismatics, then buying them directly with shards is more effective
[2018-12-26 19:27] awaclus: flipping extra in the armory is only worth it in rare cases, e.g. when you're just a tiny bit off of a thing that you want

[2018-12-27 08:19] amalloy: there doesn't need to be a turn limit, since they have a proof that all games lead to repetition or elimination
[2018-12-27 08:20] namington: do we know that all games lead to an endstate in less than 2^32 or whatever turns though?
[2018-12-27 08:21] namington: ie, is there a known upper bound on game length
[2018-12-27 08:23] amalloy: i guess we'd have to know what counts as a repetition to establish that
[2018-12-27 08:23] amalloy: how many turns in a row can players pass without harvesting drones before it offers a draw
[2018-12-27 08:24] namington: It's not like players are forced to accept a draw either
[2018-12-27 08:24] namington: Like theoretically let's say both players sat there
[2018-12-27 08:24] namington: Just hitting spacebar
[2018-12-27 08:25] namington: Or maybe clicking a drone and buying something inconsequential to throw off some anti-botting thing if applicable
[2018-12-27 08:25] amalloy: sure, but you also don't know that the server will give up on any fixed turn. of course elyot thought ahead and uses arbitrary-precision ints for the turn counter
[2018-12-27 08:25] namington: Yeah you're right, it may not be a fixed turn
[2018-12-27 08:25] amalloy: obviously it eventually runs out of RAM and crashes
[2018-12-27 08:25] amalloy: but that's "draw by disconnect"
[2018-12-27 08:25] namington: Maybe the game just develops a memory leak and crashes
[2018-12-27 08:26] namington: Top prismata players have discovered that the optimal move is Q shift D space space every turn
[2018-12-27 08:26] namington: Games are decided by who has more RAM in their machine
[2018-12-27 08:27] namington: "I didn't even know overclocking RAM was possible", says expert
[2018-12-27 08:27] amalloy: btw i don't agree with "Or maybe clicking a drone and buying something inconsequential to throw off some anti-botting thing if applicable". if you ever have to buy something inconsequential to prevent the server from kicking you out, that's a reasonably low limit on turncount
[2018-12-27 08:28] amalloy: because supply limit is pretty small, as ints go, and you run out of stuff
[2018-12-27 08:28] namington: Yeah I guess you're right
[2018-12-27 08:28] namington: Even if you only get like 1 gold every 5 turns
[2018-12-27 08:28] namington: That's still a small number
[2018-12-27 08:28] namington: Relative to how big ints can get

[2019-01-05 17:04] deadhour: Would Thermite Core be too OP if it didn't have Lifespan?
[2019-01-05 17:05] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): the main reason for lifespan is to prevent having unlimited units on the board
[2019-01-05 17:05] wilson0862 (Wilson): That's never going to happen for unit limit reasons, for one.
[2019-01-05 17:05] wilson0862 (Wilson): This is why I don't blitz.
[2019-01-05 17:05] awaclus: Thermite Core would also be OP though
[2019-01-05 17:05] kristian1103 (Arkanishu): well, thermite core already is kinda op
[2019-01-05 17:06] awaclus: the fact that it only has 5 lifespan instead of 6-8 is a substantial nerf even
[2019-01-05 17:06] awaclus: having 8 vs not having any would probably be roughly as strong
[2019-01-05 17:07] awaclus: like eventually there always comes a point where you want more than 8 attack even if you have cores
[2019-01-05 17:08] awaclus: but with the current core, that point is largely determined by when the oldest core dies
[2019-01-05 17:09] jamberine: I wouldn't say that's necessarily true
[2019-01-05 17:09] jamberine: at sufficiently low econ they can't afford to defend 8 a turn anyway
[2019-01-05 17:11] jamberine: I would say 8 to 5 is a much bigger difference
[2019-01-05 17:12] jamberine: I think there's a lot of struggling for defense with current thermite core because of the absorb denial
[2019-01-05 17:13] jamberine: so I think people will break more often in the 3 turns from 5 to 8 than in the infitely many turns after
